page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dual AMD EPYC 9575F processors. This x86_64 architecture system provides 128 physical cores and 256 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 3.3 GHz with 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core.

The server features an integrated accelerator payload consisting of eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 1536 GB of high-bandwidth video memory. Local storage is provided by 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, supporting high-throughput data access directly on the physical machine. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

Given the high density of Blackwell GPUs, massive system memory, and extensive NVMe storage, this bare metal instance represents a highly consolidated hardware profile. It is designed for resource-intensive workloads such as large language model training, complex deep learning tasks, high-performance computing, and massive database operations that require direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
description: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a high-density bare metal server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 256 vCPUs across 128 physical AMD EPYC 9575F cores running at 3.3 GHz with 2.0 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 3072.0 GB of system memory, offering 12.0 GB per core, and a substantial local storage array of 28608 GB NVMe SSDs. For acceleration, the server integrates eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. This bare metal configuration is designed to eliminate virtualization overhead, offering direct hardware access for demanding computational tasks. It is highly suited for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, massive database hosting, and high-performance computing applications requiring significant parallel processing power, high-speed local storage throughput, and extensive memory capacity.
og_description: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 256 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 3072 GB RAM, 28608 GB NVMe SSD storage, and eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 1536 GB total VRAM.

tagline: A bare metal accelerator platform featuring eight Blackwell GPUs, massive system memory, and high-capacity local NVMe storage for intensive parallel computing.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processors with 256 vCPUs and 128 physical cores.
Equipped with eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing 1536 GB of total VRAM.
Includes 3072.0 GB of system memory and 28608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Bare metal deployment eliminates virtualization overhead for direct hardware execution.
Optimized for large-scale AI training, deep learning, and massive database workloads.
